title: Andorhal
timelinePosition: Directly after Chapter 6. The party reaches Andorhal after Arthas's dismissal and gets inside before the city realizes it is under investigation.
openLocation: Andorhal's gates and market district, with inspection offices, granaries, and the mage quarter beyond.
transport: Arrive on Thalassian horses; move on foot once inside.
immediateObjective: Get into Andorhal under cover, learn how the plague is being planted at the city's center, and map the cult traffic moving it.
constraints: No Kel'Thuzad naming in this chapter.
No certainty that the Barovs built the plague cult.
No suggestion that the old seller / newer grain-man clue is solved.
No warm trust language beyond plain working reliance.

povKnowsAtOpen: Grain is carrying the poison, and every trail of rot still leads toward Andorhal.
Millhaven was the first village fully taken. Too much of the country beyond it shows the same weapon hidden inside ordinary sickness.
Jaina can feel the plague before local officials know what they are looking at, but they are still chasing shipments that have already landed instead of stopping the spread at its source.
Barov names keep showing up in the paperwork, but a name on a deed is not guilt.
The grain-side farewell pattern tied to the old seller clue is still thin, still unresolved, and not worth turning away from Andorhal for.
Arthas heard the warning and chose the war everyone could see.
povDoesNotKnowAtOpen: How the poison is worked into grain inside a working trade hub.
What the living body adds after ingestion that the grain alone cannot explain.
How many hidden cultists operate in Andorhal.
Whether the Barovs built this, helped it, or are only useful cover for someone else's work.
Who designed the plague or who sits above the cult.
mustNotBeImpliedYet: No Kel'Thuzad naming in this chapter.
No certainty that the Barovs built the plague cult.
No suggestion that the old seller / newer grain-man clue is solved.
No warm trust language beyond plain working reliance.
castKnowsLeavingChapter: Andorhal is a working mill city, not an already-dead one.
The poison is worked into the grain during packaging by skilled alchemical and necromantic hands.
The granary district is hiding cultists, not random saboteurs.
Corin has earned his place with them. Havel keeps trying to drag the problem back into militia habits and onto routes he controls, and something about that pull feels wrong.
They can still take someone alive that night.

pov: She opens thinking Andorhal can be solved like a Farstrider hunt: scout harder, find the weak gate, read the ground better than the people who live there.

That approach does not work here. Too many clerks, too many rules, too many mages, too much grain moving under ordinary hands. The poison moves through daily traffic, not through some camp she can outflank.
She keeps looking for the clean field answer, and every answer she finds is only half of one.
The full picture only starts to come together when the work splits and Jaina takes the doors Sylvanas cannot open by stealth or force.
By chapter close Sylvanas no longer thinks of Jaina as help she happened to bring along. She cannot pretend otherwise after what Jaina finds. No tenderness needed.
Chapter 8 opens with Sylvanas ready to divide the work by skill and let Jaina finish her half without a minder.

beats: Jaina already proved useful on the road, but she was easier to trust when Sylvanas could watch every step of the work.
Here Jaina keeps finding answers Sylvanas cannot pull out of streets, roofs, or frightened faces, and she does it without showing off or asking leave.
Sylvanas can think Jaina's name plainly now. What distance remains comes from caution and pressure, not contempt.

team.label: partnership
beats: The group still moves at Sylvanas's pace, but the reliance is growing and neither woman bothers to name it.
The work truly splits here. Each has to trust the other to finish the part she cannot watch herself.
Keep the respect practical and dry, never sentimental.
They are not softened. They are simply harder to pull apart from the same job.

entries.rule: Still with the scouting pack and alive through this chapter.
Introduced here as a local Kirin Tor contact inside Andorhal.
Introduced here as a local militia contact whose city-first caution, need for control, and habit of steering people through his own routes obstruct the raid and may hide something worse.
Not present. She remains on the northbound warning line after escorting the survivor girl out in Chapter 3.
Background only. Keep it weak and unresolved.

castAndHandoffRules.chapterHandoffTarget: Chapter 8 opens the same night in Andorhal with Corin inside their circle, Havel pushing for a slower cleaner move through routes he controls, and the ambush going forward anyway.

scenes.title: Into the Mouth
scenes.sceneFunction: Get through Andorhal's gate under cover, then learn enough local truth to stop circling and start moving.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: The horses are stabled outside the wall while the party approaches on foot with Jaina's cover paperwork.
Arthas's refusal is still fresh, so the party expects truth to come from the city and its labor rather than from authority.
Grain is already confirmed as the carrier, but not how Andorhal is altering it from inside the hub.
scenes.arcPressure: Sylvanas's first instinct is to solve Andorhal like a gate, patrol, and choke-point problem, but the scene immediately pushes her toward clerks, papers, and local intermediaries instead.

enrichment.detail: Canon places Andorhal as Lordaeron's main agricultural distribution point east of Lordamere Lake, with grain moving out along the King's Road to northern settlements. That supports backed-up wagons, inspection pressure, and the feeling that grain traffic is the city's circulatory system rather than background color.

Jaina is canonically Antonidas's apprentice and a Kirin Tor plague investigator. That makes her cover paperwork and later access to mage-side rooms feel institutionally plausible instead of convenient.
enrichment.source: Warcraft Wiki: Jaina Proudmoore — https://warcraft.wiki.gg/wiki/Jaina_Proudmoore; Warcraft Wiki: Antonidas — https://warcraft.wiki.gg/wiki/Antonidas

beats.goal: Get the party through Andorhal's gate without exposing the real investigation.
beats.conflict: Recent grain-focused scrutiny means clerks, seals, and guards are reading granary traffic harder than a normal trade stop.

outcome.summary: Jaina's cover holds and they get inside, but Sylvanas immediately sees that grain movement is already under tighter watch.

beats.valueShift: approach under cover -> inside a living city already braced around grain
newOnPageInformation: Andorhal is still a working market city.
Grain-district traffic is under new scrutiny.
Jaina's cover holds.
Sylvanas marks guard patterns, choke points, exits, and the flow through the gate.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether more city officials have started noticing the threat or the cult has tightened its own screen.
Which officials are only overworked clerks and which belong to something darker.
sensoryAnchors: boot-packed mud at the threshold
grain dust in the cobbles
ink-cuffed clerk shaking from overwork
wagons backed up by the new checks
beats.transitionIntent: question-pressure: the tightened grain screen makes local contacts urgent.
beats.verifierNotes: Andorhal's false normalcy is already established by chapter6's horizon reveal. The new work here is the gate-level scrutiny and lived entry pressure, not the city's basic aliveness.

beats.goal: Turn Corin and Havel's partial truths into one usable local picture before the city closes around them.
beats.conflict: The evidence is split between street observation and paperwork, while Havel keeps pressing delay, procedure, and routes he controls.

outcome.summary: Corin's records and Havel's observations align enough to justify action, but Havel's control instinct stays unresolved pressure inside the room.

beats.valueShift: inside a living city already braced around grain -> Andorhal has local witnesses and buried warnings
newOnPageInformation: Havel has watched sickness gather around the grain system for months.
A new supplier pattern entered roughly three months ago.
Some granary workers move with the wrong kind of discipline, too still and too watchful, often in pairs.
Havel's reports go nowhere.
Corin has been tracking tainted shipments for weeks with no response.
Corin's records match what Havel sees from the street.
The lack of any answer from Dalaran could come from stupidity, indifference, or something worse.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who directs the disciplined workers.
Whether Havel is merely cautious or steering them on purpose.
Whether Corin has seen the ritual itself or only what it leaves behind.
sensoryAnchors: street-dusted militia leather
scarred hands near a well-used truncheon
ink-stained fingers and bruised eyes
ignored notices weighted by a cracked river stone
beats.transitionIntent: decision-carry: once the street truth and paper truth align, the fastest move is to split the work.
beats.plantedThread: Havel keeps trying to drag the investigation back into procedure and onto routes he controls.
beats.verifierModification: Calibrated the chapter-exit implications so Corin can enter the trust boundary while Havel remains unresolved pressure. This matches the outline's suspicion lane and preserves Chapter 8's handoff.
beats.verifierNotes: Corin and Havel are first-on-page here. Give both full introduction weight; only the broader pattern of ignored warnings is established.

scenes.title: The Split
scenes.sceneFunction: The job in Andorhal forces the investigation to split. Jaina follows the arcane trail through records and granaries while Sylvanas tracks the human side from the street, then catches a cult handler making a dead drop.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Corin and Havel have separately confirmed tainted shipments, changed suppliers, and disciplined workers in the granary district.
The party is inside the city and running on borrowed secrecy.
Jaina can open inspection offices and magical archives that Sylvanas cannot usefully force.
scenes.arcPressure: This is the real test of Sylvanas's control instinct: she has to let Jaina take the half of the city Sylvanas cannot personally dominate while she commits to the street hunt herself.

enrichment.detail: Andorhal should read as low-walled, practical grain infrastructure: granaries, loading yards, manifests, wagons, clerks, and warm grain dust everywhere. The horror works best when labor noise and logistics stay normal on the surface.

At this stage the grain carries the outer shell while repeated ingestion conditions the body, which is why handlers protecting themselves from treated grain matters more than harvest-country fear. Distribution sites, not farms, are where the mechanism becomes catastrophic.

beats.dilemma: Keep the party physically together for control, or split by aptitude and risk danger arriving first where Sylvanas cannot watch it.
beats.decision: Sylvanas sends Jaina with Corin into the mage-side records while she takes Havel and the street hunt through the granary district.
beats.valueShift: Andorhal has local witnesses and buried warnings -> the job becomes two simultaneous hunts
newOnPageInformation: Jaina can work unsupervised in inspection offices and magical archives.
Sylvanas reads the street faster and harder than anyone else in the party.
The split is by skill, not rank.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether anyone has already reacted to their presence.
Which side of the split will hit real danger first.
sensoryAnchors: district notes spread against a warehouse wall
two fingers held flat like a knife
access seals fumbled in a hurry
the four-woman pack splitting without discussion
beats.transitionIntent: action-split: both lanes begin at once under one shrinking clock.
beats.verifierNotes: The split is new in scale, not in direction. It grows out of the already-established shared method and outward-facing alignment from chapters 5-6.

beats.goal: Work the granary district on foot with Havel and read the human traffic for the pattern underneath.
beats.conflict: The district is dense with ordinary labor, broken sightlines, and workers trained to disappear into routine. Havel gives real names and real shifts but keeps steering the walk through routes he controls.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas reads at least eight people who move wrong — too still, too disciplined, often in pairs — but she cannot yet prove what they are guarding or whether Havel is territorial or covering something.

beats.valueShift: the job becomes two simultaneous hunts -> the granary district reads as a guarded operation, not random sickness
newOnPageInformation: At least eight people in the district move wrong, too still and too disciplined, often in pairs.
Workers who should be loading grain stand in patterns that read as trained waiting rather than fatigue.
Havel knows which granaries changed suppliers three months ago.
Havel's local knowledge is real, but he keeps trying to drag the work through routes he controls.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: What the disciplined workers are guarding.
Whether Havel is only territorial or actively covering something.
Where the goods are going after the granary loading lanes.
sensoryAnchors: workers whose stillness reads as trained waiting
loading lanes with too many blind corners
Havel pointing with two fingers held flat like a knife
grain dust drifting in afternoon light
beats.transitionIntent: pattern-escalation: the wrong kind of discipline in ordinary workers makes Sylvanas shift from mapping to tracking.
beats.plantedThread: Havel's route knowledge is useful, but his habit of steering them through spaces he controls remains unresolved pressure.
beats.verifierNotes: The network is already known to be organized. The new advance here is countable: eight people, paired discipline, and a three-month supplier change.

beats.goal: Follow a dead-drop handler to the treatment site and see the cell working.
beats.conflict: Three streets of tailing through a city Sylvanas does not know, with the handler moving fast and Sylvanas unable to call for backup without breaking cover.

outcome.summary: Sylvanas identifies the dead-drop chain, the secondary granary as a treatment site, and a two-layer cell — handlers running drops and workers contaminating grain — but she still does not know who sits above it or whether the hunt has already seen her.

beats.valueShift: the granary district reads as a guarded operation -> the hidden cell becomes physically visible
newOnPageInformation: The cell has two layers: handlers running dead drops and workers contaminating grain.
The dead drop proves someone is guiding the work from above the local handlers.
The handlers protect themselves from the grain they contaminate — masks, gloves, no bare skin.
The secondary granary is a treatment site, not just storage.
Off-ledger caches and movement patterns show the conspiracy has been here for months.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who sits above the local handlers.
Whether Sylvanas was spotted during the tail.
What exactly is being done to the grain inside the treatment site.
sensoryAnchors: dead drop pressed into a wall crack without a backward glance
rooftop grit and chimney soot under her hands
masked figures moving under dirty-window lamplight
gloved hands pressing something thick into open grain bags
beats.transitionIntent: proof-carry: Sylvanas now has the physical half and needs Jaina's return to explain the mechanism.
beats.verifierNotes: This is the chapter's peak action beat. The stakeout earns the physical proof that makes the later synthesis with Jaina's findings pay off.

scenes.title: The Full Picture
scenes.sceneFunction: Jaina brings the magical answer, Sylvanas brings the physical answer, and together they finally see the whole thing. Then they choose the same-night ambush.
knowledgeAtSceneStart: Jaina and Corin have worked the inspection offices while Sylvanas has mapped the granary district and the dead-drop chain.
Both halves return with the cult's next treatment shift still ahead of them but not by much.
Corin and Havel are both inside the room, but not equally trusted.
scenes.arcPressure: The chapter's stance shift lands here: Sylvanas can only act cleanly once she admits that neither her field read nor Jaina's arcane read is enough alone.

enrichment.detail: At this stage of the field plague, grain carries the outer shell, repeated ingestion conditions the body, and death completes the lock. Jaina can identify layered design and high-arcanum handling here without yet owning the final undeath answer.

Antonidas is Grand Magus of the Kirin Tor and Jaina's mentor, which makes Corin's ignored reports and access-seal work read as institutional delay under real hierarchy rather than a free-floating local office problem.

beats.goal: Merge Jaina's mage-side findings and Sylvanas's street proof into one operational picture.
beats.conflict: Each half explains only part of the mechanism, and the full scale of a designed plague — not accidental contamination — lands in the room.

outcome.summary: The mechanism and local cell finally align into one answer — high-arcanum necromantic carrier layer, host body finishes the work, skilled handlers protecting themselves — but Andorhal still reads like one hub, not the top of the system.

beats.valueShift: the hidden cell becomes physically visible -> the city's packaging-stage mechanism is finally legible
newOnPageInformation: The grain is treated during packaging with a necromantic carrier layer.
The host body finishes the work after repeated ingestion, so the grain alone is not the whole mechanism.
The design requires high-arcanum knowledge, not hedge magic.
The cult protects itself from its own weapon during handling.
The local cell has discipline, supply lines, and command structure.
Neither half of the investigation would have been enough alone.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Who designed the process.
Whether Andorhal is the top of the system or only one hub in the chain.
How the cursed victim becomes undead after death.
Whether the cult already suspects them.
sensoryAnchors: Jaina's neat notes beside hand-drawn route marks
two kinds of evidence landing on one table
the room going quiet when the scale lands
beats.transitionIntent: social-pressure: once both halves fit, the room becomes about who can be trusted to act on it.
disclosureProvenance: Jaina and Corin bring archive, shipment, and arcane-work findings.
Sylvanas brings rooftop surveillance, dead-drop mapping, and handler behavior.
beats.verifierModification: Reframed the mechanism synthesis as a returned report over the shared table so Jaina's offscreen work lands through Sylvanas's reception of it, preserving limited-third POV while keeping Jaina's findings load-bearing.
beats.verifierNotes: Packaging-stage contamination, host-body participation, and high-arcanum design all extend established plague knowledge. The synthesis is the chapter's intellectual payoff.

beats.dilemma: Corin is moving toward the danger and has been right about everything so far. Havel keeps pressing safer routes, dawn timing, and militia control. Both men want to help — but one of them wants control more than safety.
beats.decision: Corin earns his place inside the circle simply by being right and by choosing speed over self-preservation. Havel stays useful but stays outside the trust boundary.
beats.valueShift: the city's packaging-stage mechanism is finally legible -> the room draws a line between who can be trusted to move and who cannot
newOnPageInformation: Corin has earned a place inside the trust boundary.
Havel keeps pressing safer lanes, dawn timing, and routes only he controls.
Something about Havel's insistence on controlling the route feels wrong even before anyone can prove why.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Corin or Havel have already been marked as leaks by the cult.
Whether Havel's route-pushing is fear or active cover.
sensoryAnchors: Corin's access notes spread flat and ready
Havel redrawing the same safer lanes again and again
Sylvanas watching the route choices instead of the words
beats.transitionIntent: decision-pressure: once the trust line is drawn, delay becomes its own risk.
beats.plantedThread: Havel's insistence on exits and routes he controls stays unresolved going into the raid.
beats.verifierNotes: Corin earning trust and Havel staying outside the boundary is the chapter's key social result. Do not merge with the evidence synthesis — this beat lands through watching two men react differently to the same danger.

beats.dilemma: Wait for dawn and formal militia control, or strike that night before the cell scrubs the site and vanishes back into routine.
beats.decision: Sylvanas, backed by Jaina and Corin, commits the team to a same-night capture at the secondary granary during the next treatment shift.
beats.valueShift: the city's packaging-stage mechanism is finally legible -> the hunt narrows to one live capture tonight
newOnPageInformation: A live capture is possible tonight because the cult still feels secure.
Jaina's magical read can test whatever a prisoner says.
Cyndia and Lyriel are strong enough on perimeter to let the leads commit.
Corin knows which doors open at night.
Havel keeps pressing routes and ways out only he controls.
stillUnknownAfterBeat: Whether Corin or Havel have already been marked as leaks.
Whether Havel's route-pushing is fear or cover.
How fast the cult can scatter once it senses contact.
sensoryAnchors: candlelit maps weighted with knives and keys
chalk marks over door routes
two leads aligned across the table
Havel trying to drag the plan back into his geometry
beats.transitionIntent: action-continuation: Chapter 8 opens on the grab at the next treatment shift.
beats.plantedThread: Havel's insistence on exits and routes he controls stays unresolved going into the raid.
beats.verifierNotes: The same-night capture plan is a new tactical thread, not a callback. Havel's route pressure stays unresolved on purpose; do not treat the room as having settled him into trust.

factsLocked: Andorhal is a working mill city, not another dead village.
The contamination is introduced during packaging by skilled dark alchemy and necromancy, and the cult protects itself from its own weapon during handling.
The granary district holds a hidden cult network with at least eight people in it and two layers to its command.
Corin is a trusted local ally inside Andorhal. Havel knows the city and keeps trying to steer the operation into routes he controls.
The next move is a same-night capture of a living zealot at the secondary granary.
The old seller / newer grain-man clue remains weak, background, and unresolved.
factsNotEarned: Proof that the wider spread is already complete.
Any collapse of the partnership into overt warmth.

warnings: Opening investigation beats all rise through different info channels; prose pacing will need strong differentiation between gate pressure, Corin contact, and Havel contact.
The investigative core leans heavily yes-but; local wins need distinct texture so progress does not blur.

chapterBridge: Direct carry from chapter6's horizon reveal: the same four riders come off Arthas's refusal straight into covert entry, with the city's false normalcy, the repeated grain-road farewell pattern, and Jaina's city-first trust all still active before the gate even opens.

craftResearch.findings: Investigative chapters stay alive when each scene functions like a domino: one goal, one friction pattern, and one changed result that knocks the next move into place. That standard favored gate pressure -> local witness alignment -> split hunt -> merged proof -> tactical decision instead of repeating one clue mode.

After a pressure cluster, the story needs a reaction-dilemma-decision pocket so momentum sharpens instead of blurring. Beat 3 earns the split by decision, and beat 6 earns the raid by converting proof into a concrete next move.

A strong chapter opening hooks fast and orients the reader quickly around POV, place, and immediate tension. That supported keeping chapter7 at the gate under live scrutiny rather than reopening Arthas debate or summarizing the road again.

Chapter endings should leave a live unanswered question, revelation, or action hook rather than a closed summary. That validated ending on the same-night capture plan with Havel still unresolved and the city ready to answer back.

pacing.notes: The chapter opens in immediate pressure, varies its scene functions cleanly, and uses the two sequel beats as genuine breathing-and-decision pockets instead of stalls. The tension rises from covert entry to converging proof to a same-night strike commitment without flattening into one long discovery scene.

structure.notes: The opening drops the reader into active gate scrutiny, the midpoint turn lands when the hidden cell becomes physically legible, and the close plants a clear unresolved next move. The chapter also keeps an action-lean 4/2 scene-sequel split that fits a dark investigative chapter.

character.notes: Sylvanas remains active in every beat, makes the key split and raid decisions, and earns the arc-position shift from control to skill-based trust. Jaina, Corin, and Havel all serve distinct functions: missing-half specialist, trusted local archive ally, and unresolved route-pressure contact.

transitions.notes: All 6 beat transitionIntent chains cohere and scene boundaries land cleanly: gate pressure drives contact urgency, contact alignment drives the split, street proof demands Jaina's mechanism read, and merged proof forces the raid decision. No missing intents required addition.

continuity.notes: 2 beats are new and 4 are evolutions. No contradictions were found in names, geography, prior relationship state, or active thread handling. The main continuity correction was keeping Havel outside the trust boundary so the chapter remains aligned with the outline and chapter8 handoff.

modifications.description: Corrected the chapter-exit social state so Corin enters the trust boundary while Havel remains useful but unresolved pressure. This preserves the outline's suspicion lane and keeps chapter8 from inheriting a false trust reset.
Reframed beat 5 so Jaina's mechanism findings arrive through Sylvanas's on-page reception of the report instead of implying an offscreen POV slip.
Added a direct-carry chapterBridge tying chapter6's horizon reveal and Arthas refusal to chapter7's covert gate entry, preserving pressure and chapter seam continuity.
